project.title: ¿Qué pasaría si la Independencia hubiera fracasado?
project.tone: Contrafactual histórico dramático tipo documental '¿qué pasaría si…?'. La NARRACIÓN va en TERCERA persona (narrador histórico), NUNCA en segunda persona ('tú') ni primera plural ('nos/nuestro'), y NUNCA menciona al esqueleto. Se puede nombrar a Miguel Hidalgo y el Grito de Dolores (figura histórica, retrato respetuoso, escenario claramente hipotético). Final ABIERTO (sin pregunta y sin anunciar Parte 2).
project.render_notes: Huesito VARÍA como tres personajes (todos character_edited sobre la misma base): huesito_cura (cura insurgente estilo Hidalgo, homenaje/calaca, NO retrato real), huesito_insurgente (combatiente con machete/fusil), huesito_colonial (súbdito del presente alterno). Coherencia: Huesito siempre VIVO — cráneo de hueso marfil limpio y pulido, grandes ojos café vivos y expresivos, NUNCA Pixar/clínico y NUNCA un zombi/no-muerto/yokai. image_prompt Y animation_prompt LARGOS y ULTRA-detallados que COMPLEMENTAN ('the provided…', cámara distinta por escena, KEY ACTION first 2s, 'keep… EXACTLY as provided'). SIN gore ni sangre (la derrota se muestra con humo, el estandarte que cae y la postura). Gente mexicana DIVERSA (morena, cabello negro). Salto de tiempo con carteles '1810' y '200 años después' dichos por la voz. Cierre ABIERTO con la semilla de una nueva rebelión organizándose en secreto.
project.series: Huesito · ¿Qué pasaría si…? (autoconclusivo con FINAL ABIERTO; posible Parte 2 si tiene buen recibimiento — NO se anuncia en el video)

huesito.description: Esqueleto semi-realista, cráneo de hueso marfil envejecido y texturizado con poros y grietas finas, dientes creíbles, grandes ojos redondos color café muy vivos y expresivos, complexión alta y esbelta. Personaje base pre-existente: NO se genera, solo se usa como referencia.

ingredients.edit_prompt: Dress the provided base skeleton as a Mexican insurgent priest of 1810 in the style of Miguel Hidalgo (a respectful skeleton homage, not a realistic portrait). Outfit, described exactly: a long black cassock (sotana) of heavy worn wool buttoned down the front, a simple white clerical collar, a dark knee-length frock coat over it with tarnished buttons, a wide black low-crowned hat hanging at the back, a plain wooden rosary and a small brass crucifix at the chest; sturdy worn leather shoes; one bony hand raised as if calling out, the other gripping a thick rope tied to an old bronze church bell. Aged-wool, linen and brass textures, sober blacks and whites with warm candle-lit tones, no readable text, no logos. Plain neutral light-grey studio background, soft even three-point lighting, full body, front 3/4 view. Keep the EXACT clean polished ivory-bone skull with fine cracks, the very large round and EXPRESSIVE LIVING brown eyes, and the tall slim proportions of the provided base — a warm, fervent, living skeleton, NOT an undead, NOT a zombie, NOT a skeletal yokai, NOT decayed, grey or scary. No scene, no text.

Dress the provided base skeleton as a humble Mexican insurgent fighter of 1810. Outfit, described exactly: a loose off-white coarse cotton shirt (camisa de manta) with rolled sleeves, baggy beige cotton trousers tied with a woven sash, a faded red-and-brown striped wool poncho (jorongo) over one shoulder, a wide straw hat (sombrero) tipped back, worn leather huarache sandals, a leather cartridge pouch on a strap; one bony hand gripping a worn machete with a wooden handle, an old flintlock musket slung on the back. Coarse cotton, wool and leather textures, earthy off-whites, browns and faded reds, dusty and worn, no readable text, no logos. Plain neutral light-grey studio background, soft even three-point lighting, full body, front 3/4 view. Keep the EXACT clean polished ivory-bone skull with fine cracks, the very large round and EXPRESSIVE LIVING brown eyes, and the tall slim proportions of the provided base — a warm, fierce, living skeleton, NOT an undead, NOT a zombie, NOT a skeletal yokai, NOT decayed, grey or scary. No scene, no text.

Dress the provided base skeleton as an ordinary colonial subject in a bleak alternate present where Mexico is still a Spanish colony — muted, timeless colonial-servant clothing. Outfit, described exactly: a plain coarse grey-brown linen tunic-shirt buttoned to the neck, a drab dark waistcoat, simple straight grey trousers, a worn long hooded cloak (capote) of undyed wool draped over the shoulders, plain worn leather shoes; muted, colorless, humble and faintly oppressed in feel; one bony hand clutching the edge of the cloak. Drab linen, wool and leather textures, washed-out greys and browns, joyless, no readable text, no logos. Plain neutral light-grey studio background, soft even three-point lighting, full body, front 3/4 view. Keep the EXACT clean polished ivory-bone skull with fine cracks, the very large round and EXPRESSIVE LIVING brown eyes (here quietly wistful), and the tall slim proportions of the provided base — a warm, living skeleton, NOT an undead, NOT a zombie, NOT a skeletal yokai, NOT decayed, grey-fleshed or scary. No scene, no text.

ingredients.generation_prompt: A crowd of ordinary Mexican insurgents of 1810, adult men and women with brown skin and dark hair, photoreal. Described exactly: humble campesinos and townsfolk in coarse off-white cotton shirts (camisa de manta), baggy cotton trousers and woven sashes, faded striped wool ponchos and rebozos, wide straw hats and leather huarache sandals, some in simple long skirts and blouses; armed with worn machetes, wooden clubs, farm tools (pitchforks, scythes) and a few old flintlock muskets; fierce, hopeful, determined faces. Coarse cotton, wool and leather textures, earthy off-whites, browns and faded reds, dusty. Surging, marching stances. Plain neutral grey background, soft even lighting, full body group. Diverse working people, brown skin, dark hair, no logos, no readable text. Only adults.

A group of three royalist Spanish colonial soldiers of 1810, adult men, photoreal. Described exactly: disciplined troops in well-kept military uniforms — white cross-belts over dark navy-blue regimental coats with red facings, cuffs and brass buttons, white breeches, tall black shako or tricorn hats, black leather boots and gaiters; each holding a long flintlock musket with a fixed bayonet, a sabre at the hip, a cartridge box on the belt. Crisp wool, brass and leather textures, deep navy, white and red, orderly and imposing. Firm, advancing military stances. Plain neutral grey background, soft even lighting, full body group. No real emblems or modern logos, no readable text. Only adult men.

A single tall insurgent banner of the 1810 Mexican uprising, photoreal. Described exactly: a weathered rectangular cloth banner mounted on a worn wooden pole, bearing a venerated image of the Virgin of Guadalupe in faded warm colors (the historic standard of the insurgents) within a simple painted border; the cloth frayed and battle-worn, edges tattered. Aged painted-cloth and wood textures, warm faded reds, golds, greens and blues. Held upright. Plain neutral grey background, soft even lighting, full object. Respectful historic depiction, no modern logos, no readable text.

A single large Spanish colonial royal flag for an alternate present, photoreal. Described exactly: a tall banner of the colonial Spanish crown — a white field bearing the red ragged Cross of Burgundy (crossed red saltire branches) and a crowned royal coat of arms in crimson and gold, mounted on a tall metal pole, heavy fabric rippling. Rich heavy fabric texture, deep reds, whites and golds, imperial and austere. Plain neutral grey background, soft even lighting, full object. Generic historic royal banner, no modern logos, no readable text.

The small Mexican town of Dolores at dawn in 1810, empty of main characters. A modest colonial stone church with a bell tower and a single bronze bell, a dusty plaza with low whitewashed adobe houses, wooden doors and clay-tiled roofs, a few simple market stalls, soft golden dawn light breaking over distant hills, faint mist. Humble, historic, charged with the dawn of an uprising. Empty plaza staging area, no main characters, photorealistic, wide composition. No readable text, no modern elements, no logos.

An open Mexican countryside battlefield road in 1810, empty of main characters. A wide dirt road cutting through dry golden fields and scrubland under a vast sky, low rolling hills, scattered agave and cactus, a few distant trees, dust hanging in the air, a moody dramatic daylight. Epic, raw, historic. Empty staging area, no main characters, photorealistic, wide cinematic composition. No readable text, no modern elements, no logos.

The vast central plaza (Zocalo) of a great colonial Mexican city in a bleak alternate present where it is still a Spanish colony, empty of main characters. A huge stone plaza framed by an imposing baroque colonial palace and a great colonial cathedral, austere and grand, tall flagpoles in the foreground, an overcast desaturated grey sky, a cold oppressive imperial atmosphere with no festive decoration of any kind. Empty plaza staging area, no main characters, photorealistic, wide cinematic composition. No readable text, no modern brand logos.

The narrow streets of a great colonial Mexican city in a bleak alternate present still under Spanish colonial rule, empty of main characters. Drab cobblestone streets lined with austere baroque colonial stone buildings, barred windows, heavy wooden doors, faded royal banners hanging limp, no color or celebration anywhere, a cold desaturated grey overcast light, a joyless oppressive mood. Empty street staging area, no main characters, photorealistic, wide composition. No readable text, no modern brand logos.

The dim interior of a hidden back room in a colonial-era building at night, empty of main characters. A cramped candle-lit room with rough plastered walls, a worn wooden table scattered with papers and a single flickering candle, shuttered windows, shadows pooling in the corners, a faint hidden glimpse of green-white-red cloth tucked at the table's edge — a secret, conspiratorial, charged atmosphere. Empty room staging area, no main characters, photorealistic, wide composition. No readable text, no modern logos.

voiceover.text: [curious] ¿Qué pasaría si la Independencia de México hubiera fracasado y el país nunca se hubiera liberado de la corona española?

visual.image_prompt: Using the provided insurgent priest (Huesito as Miguel Hidalgo) on the provided 1810 Dolores town plate: KEY camera = a rousing low hero shot at dawn, looking up at him on the church steps. The provided Huesito priest hauls on the rope of the old bronze bell with one hand and throws the other arm up to the sky, mouth open in a great rallying cry, the golden dawn breaking behind the bell tower, dust and light around him, the humble plaza waking below. Stirring, historic, goosebump energy. Huesito stays clearly himself and ALIVE: a clean polished ivory-bone skull with fine cracks, very large round and EXPRESSIVE LIVING brown eyes, tall slim proportions — a warm, expressive, living skeleton, NOT an undead, NOT a zombie, NOT a skeletal yokai, NOT decayed, grey or scary. Semi-realistic skeleton (NOT Pixar/cartoonish/clinical); the human crowds and soldiers are realistic diverse adults of the period (the Mexican people have brown skin and dark hair); only adults, no minors; NO gore and no blood (defeat shown only through smoke, a falling banner and posture, never injuries); historically evocative, banners period-appropriate, no modern brand logos; 9:16 vertical, no text.

capcut_export.music_notes: Tambor histórico + cuerdas; épica esperanzada en el levantamiento (1-3); tensión y golpe trágico en la derrota (4-6); ambiente frío, vacío y opresivo en el presente colonial (7-14); susurro de tensión y chispa final esperanzada (15-16).
capcut_export.sfx_notes: Campana de iglesia y grito (1); marcha y multitud (2-3); descargas de mosquete y humo, sin sangre (4-5); viento frío y eco en la colonia (7-13); vela crepitando, susurros y papeles (15-16).

tts_export.full_script: [curious] ¿Qué pasaría si la Independencia de México hubiera fracasado y el país nunca se hubiera liberado de la corona española? [determined] Año 1810: Miguel Hidalgo toca la campana y lanza el Grito de Dolores que enciende a todo un pueblo. El pueblo se levanta en armas: campesinos con machetes, palos y viejos fusiles marchan por la libertad. La marcha insurgente crece y crece por los caminos, imparable, cargando su estandarte con fe y esperanza. [tense] Pero el ejército realista, mejor armado y entrenado, los enfrenta de golpe en campo abierto. [sad] Y la rebelión es aplastada: el estandarte cae entre el humo, y la esperanza se apaga. Hidalgo y sus líderes son capturados, y el sueño de un México libre muere antes de nacer. Doscientos años después, la historia es otra: México nunca nació, y todo sigue siendo la Nueva España. En el corazón del Zócalo ya no ondea el tricolor, sino la bandera colonial de la corona española. La gente todavía paga tributo a una corona lejana, y un virrey gobierna desde el viejo palacio. Aquí no existen los mexicanos: solo súbditos de la colonia, y el orgullo de una nación nunca nació. Las fiestas, el Grito, el tricolor… nada de eso existe; un quince de septiembre cualquiera, en silencio. Hasta el idioma y las costumbres son otros; la identidad de todo un pueblo simplemente se borró. Un súbdito cualquiera camina por esas calles grises, sin imaginar siquiera todo lo que pudo haber sido. Porque todo lo que conoce un pueblo —la bandera, el nombre, el país— colgó de un solo grito en la madrugada. [whispering] Pero en las sombras de esa colonia, unos cuantos empiezan a juntarse en secreto, en voz muy baja. [hopeful] Porque aunque pasen siglos bajo otra bandera, el espíritu de rebeldía nunca muere del todo…
